Class SudokuGrid

java.lang.Object
sudoku.SudokuGrid
All Implemented Interfaces:
Cloneable, ISudokuGrid
public class SudokuGrid extends Object implements ISudokuGrid
Public-facing representation of a Sudoku grid.

Wraps a SudokuGridState and adds a cached, dirty-row-invalidated string representation via the internal SudokuGrid.StringCache.

  • Field Summary

    Fields inherited from interface ISudokuGrid

  • Constructor Details

    • SudokuGrid

      public SudokuGrid()
      Initialises an empty 9x9 sudoku grid.

    • SudokuGrid

      public SudokuGrid(int size)
      Initialises an empty sudoku grid with the given size.

      A size of 9 means that each box in the sudoku grid contains the numbers 1-9.

      Sizes supported are all values x, where 2 <= x <= 16, and x is either even, square, or both.

      Parameters:
      size - The size of the sudoku grid to generate

  • Method Details

    • isValid

      public boolean isValid()
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuGrid
      Determines whether or not the current state of the sudoku grid is a valid solution so far.
      Specified by:
      isValid in interface ISudokuGrid
      Returns:
      true if the grid is valid; false if there are any incompatible cells.

    • isSolved

      public boolean isSolved()
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuGrid
      Determines whether or not the sudoku grid has yet been solved.
      Specified by:
      isSolved in interface ISudokuGrid
      Returns:
      true if the grid has been solved; false if there are any incompatible or empty cells.

    • resetGrid

      public void resetGrid()
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuGrid
      Empties the sudoku grid of values.
      Specified by:
      resetGrid in interface ISudokuGrid

    • checkForDuplicates

      public boolean checkForDuplicates(int row, int column)

    • getSize

      public int getSize()
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuGrid
      Gets the size of the grid.

      The size of a grid is determined as the range of values which it can contain.

      A grid's size can range between 2-16.

      Specified by:
      getSize in interface ISudokuGrid
      Returns:
      the size of the grid.

    • getGridDimensions

      public Tuple2<Integer,Integer> getGridDimensions()

    • getValue

      public int getValue(int row, int column)
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuGrid
      Gets the current value of a cell in a sudoku grid.

      Coordinates are indexed from (0, 0), which is the upper left-most cell in the grid.

      Specified by:
      getValue in interface ISudokuGrid
      Parameters:
      row - The row, or y-coordinate, of the cell
      column - The column, or x-coordinate, of the cell
      Returns:
      The value currently stored at the cell, or -1 if the cell is empty.

    • setValue

      public void setValue(int row, int column, int value)
      Description copied from interface: ISudokuGrid
      Sets the value of a cell in a sudoku grid.

      Coordinates are indexed from (0, 0), which is the upper left-most cell in the grid.

      Note that 1 <= value <= grid size, or to set the cell as empty, value = -1

      Specified by:
      setValue in interface ISudokuGrid
      Parameters:
      row - The row, or y-coordinate, of the cell
      column - The column, or x-coordinate, of the cell
      value - The value of the cell
